content: WebRTC signaling flow over custom transport: (1) Phone opens WebTransport bidir stream to server for signaling channel. (2) Desktop does same. (3) Server rooms them together by session/room ID. (4) Phone calls createOffer(), sets localDescription, sends SDP offer JSON through bidir stream. (5) Desktop receives offer, calls setRemoteDescription(offer), calls createAnswer(), sets localDescription, sends SDP answer back. (6) Both sides gather ICE candidates via trickle ICE: each RTCPeerConnection.onicecandidate fires as candidates are found, each candidate JSON is immediately sent through signaling bidir stream. (7) Remote side calls addIceCandidate() for each received candidate. (8) ICE selects best pair (host > srflx > relay priority). No custom signaling protocol is needed from WebRTC's perspective — any reliable ordered channel works. Using WebTransport bidir stream is correct here.
